Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine and mild hyperkalemia, suggesting impaired renal function. There is significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury, possibly with diabetic involvement. Mildly elevated ALT suggests some hepatic involvement. Further nephrological and diabetic evaluation is recommended urgently.
